meta_title: For Students: passive income to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to passive income and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Passive Income to Solve 'Can't Finish Projects'
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'can't finish' cycle by building passive income AI apps. An intermediate guide using Antigravity and Claude Code to launch your portfolio.
content.answer_block: The 'can't finish' cycle stems from scope creep and lack of market feedback. By pivoting to passive income micro-SaaS projects, you align your code with tangible value. Using Antigravity and Claude Code, you can automate the heavy lifting, shorten development cycles, and ship functional, revenue-generating tools that solidify your engineering portfolio.
content.intro:
You’ve been here before: a graveyard of GitHub repositories, half-finished landing pages, and the crushing weight of 'feature paralysis.' The problem isn't your coding ability; it’s the lack of a feedback loop. When you build for the void, motivation dies. The antidote is shifting your focus from 'coding a project' to 'engineering a passive income asset.'

By treating your code as a product that solves a specific, paid problem, you gain the discipline to finish. In this tutorial, we leverage Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for intelligent iteration. We aren't just building an app; we are building a machine that works while you study, transforming your portfolio from a list of experiments into a showcase of market-ready solutions.

steps.title: Define a High-Value Micro-Problem
steps.description: Identify a repetitive task in a niche market that users are willing to pay $10/month to solve. Focus on utility over complexity.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the top 5 pain points for freelance content creators and propose a micro-SaaS architecture that solves one via a simple API-based workflow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate a project requirements document for a micro-SaaS that automates social media scheduling'

steps.title: Rapid Scaffolding with Antigravity
steps.description: Use Antigravity to generate the boilerplate, saving hours of configuration and allowing you to focus on the business logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Next.js 14 project with Tailwind CSS, Stripe integration, and Supabase auth configured for a multi-tenant SaaS application.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'initialize the project structure based on the antigravity output'

steps.title: Engineering the Core Engine
steps.description: Build the core functionality that provides value. Keep the UI minimal so you don't get stuck on design details.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a service layer that connects the OpenAI API to a database-stored prompt library, ensuring cost-efficient token usage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'implement the core service layer using the provided architectural specs'

steps.title: Automating the Feedback Loop
steps.description: Integrate logging and analytics early so you can observe user behavior, which provides the motivation to keep finishing features.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate PostHog analytics and error logging into the Next.js API routes to track user conversion events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add telemetry to all critical user action paths'

steps.title: Deployment and Monetization
steps.description: Ship to Vercel and set up Stripe. A finished project is one that can accept a payment, regardless of how simple it is.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a deployment script for Vercel and a webhook handler for Stripe successful payments.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'configure stripe webhooks and environment variables for production deployment'

pro_tips: Always build the payment gateway before the landing page; if you can't charge for it, the project isn't worth building.
Use Claude Code to write your unit tests as you build; it prevents the 'it works on my machine' syndrome that kills momentum.
Limit your MVP scope to exactly three features; if it takes more than 48 hours to code, cut it.
Treat your codebase like a product, not a school assignment; document the 'why' behind decisions for your future portfolio interviews.
Automate your daily standup by piping your git commits into a summary file that you can use for your LinkedIn 'Building in Public' updates.
content.next_steps: Deploy your MVP to a live domain and share it in three relevant niche communities to get your first five users. Use their feedback to iterate, not to start a new project.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your best ideas sit in a local folder. You have the tools, the stack, and the roadmap to turn code into income. The only thing missing is your first commit. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
